compiled_state.title: Live-runtime validation of the langgraph runner
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
We could not exercise the live model path. Validate the runtime behaviors the
research pass flagged, on a real key, and adjust if needed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Confirm ToolStrategy structured output populates `structured_response` with real tools attached (and the None path)
- [ ] Confirm no `temperature` is sent to claude-opus-4.x (no 400)
- [ ] Verify MCP tool session lifecycle (stateless re-spawn) and a downed server doesn't silently yield no tools
- [ ] Verify Langfuse trace appears and `flush()` prevents loss in the CLI; assess prompt->trace linkage

comments.body: Partial progress: model construction + full agent assembly (model + read-only file tools + ToolStrategy structured output) validated offline for Claude (ChatAnthropic) and ChatGPT (ChatOpenAI); confirmed no temperature is sent. STILL PENDING (needs real API keys): live invoke / structured_response population, MCP session lifecycle, Langfuse trace+flush, prompt->trace linkage.
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Validation HARNESS implemented: tests/interfaces/test_llm_live.py exercises the real langgraph runner end-to-end (review_ticket + review_code) and asserts the call succeeds (no temperature 400 on claude-opus-4.x), structured_response is populated (no StructuredOutputError), and the output validates against review_result with model/runner provenance + resolved citations. Marked integration so CI excludes it (-m "not integration"); skips unless an API key + the agents extra are present. NOT yet executed live in this environment (no API keys here), so leaving in_progress: run ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... pytest -m integration to complete; Langfuse-trace + MCP-session checks need LANGFUSE_*/REBAR_LLM_MCP_SERVERS configured.
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Live validation kicked off as a dispatchable CI job: .github/workflows/llm-live.yml (workflow_dispatch) installs .[dev] and runs pytest -m integration tests/interfaces/test_llm_live.py with the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (or OPENAI_API_KEY) repo secret; it FAILS loudly if no key secret is set (so a no-key dispatch cant masquerade as a pass). Optional LANGFUSE_* secrets exercise tracing. Readiness verified in-session (no API call): the agent assembles for claude-opus-4-8, egress is open, live tests collect. TO COMPLETE: add the repo secret and trigger the workflow (Actions tab); this sandbox has no key so the actual run happens there.
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Refactored into an external-integration suite: tests live in tests/external/ marked external (new pytest marker); default CI excludes -m "not integration and not external". The manual job is now .github/workflows/external-integration.yml (pytest -m external tests/external, key secret required, fails if unset). Still needs an actual run with a repo secret to close.
comments.author: Claude

comments.body:
Optionality validation completed offline (commit 045b2d77). The hard rule — LLM stack optional for ALL interfaces (library/CLI/MCP) x ALL operations (review_ticket/review_code/scan_epics_for_spec) — is now proven by a dedicated exhaustive guard: tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py (15 tests, offline). It asserts (a) import-cleanliness for every interface entrypoint (import rebar / rebar._cli / rebar.mcp_server / rebar.llm pull NO agents stack), (b) graceful degradation per interface x operation with asserted messages (typed LLMError | CLI Error:+exit1 | gated MCP tool error), (c) an exhaustiveness check that DISCOVERS runner-backed ops from the public surface so a new op cannot ship without coverage.

Subagent review found + I fixed a real silent-success bug: scan_epics_for_spec over a zero-epic store never invoked the runner, so a missing extra returned an empty-but-successful result. Fix: cheap offline Runner.preflight() (import-only readiness check) called up front in scan_epics_for_spec and review_code; a zero-work workload now fails loudly on a missing extra. Regression test added.

The LIVE-model acceptance items in this ticket (structured_response population, no-temperature-400 on claude-opus-4.x, MCP session lifecycle, Langfuse trace+flush) still require real API keys and remain executable only via .github/workflows/external-integration.yml (pytest -m external tests/external; key secret required, fails loudly if unset) — this sandbox has no key. Those are deferred to that dispatchable CI job. The OPTIONALITY half of this ticket's intent (the user's hard requirement) is fully validated offline here. Full non-integration suite green: 1829 passed, 7 skipped.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
